sample_payloads.api_name: CoinMarketCap Fear and Greed Index API
sample_payloads.description:
Cryptocurrency market sentiment analysis tool providing Fear and Greed Index data recorded daily at 12am UTC. Access current market sentiment or historical trends within a specified date range. The Fear and Greed Index measures cryptocurrency market emotions on a 0-100 scale where lower values indicate fear (potential buying opportunities when investors oversell) and higher values indicate greed (potential market corrections when overvalued). Value classifications help interpret market conditions: Extreme Fear (1-19) suggests oversold market and potential buying opportunities, Fear (20-39) indicates cautious sentiment, Neutral (40-59) shows balanced market conditions, Greed (60-79) reflects optimistic sentiment and profit-taking opportunities, Extreme Greed (80-100) warns of potential corrections in overheated markets. Without date parameters, returns the most recent sentiment reading. With date range specified, returns all daily readings within that period for trend analysis and pattern recognition. All timestamps returned in ISO 8601 format for easy parsing and interpretation.

Parameters Usage:
- from_date: OPTIONAL. Start date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) for historical data range. If provided, to_date must also be specified. Example: "2024-01-01" retrieves data starting from January 1st, 2024.
- to_date: OPTIONAL. End date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) for historical data range. If provided, from_date must also be specified. Example: "2024-03-31" retrieves data up to March 31st, 2024. Maximum range is 365 days.
sample_payloads.name: fear_and_greed

optional.from_date: Start date for historical Fear and Greed Index data in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). When specified, retrieves daily sentiment readings from this date forward. Must be used together with to_date. The date range between from_date and to_date cannot exceed 365 days. Example: "2024-01-01" starts retrieval from January 1st, 2024. Each data point represents the sentiment recorded at 12am UTC on that date.
optional.to_date: End date for historical Fear and Greed Index data in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). When specified, retrieves daily sentiment readings up to this date. Must be used together with from_date. The date range between from_date and to_date cannot exceed 365 days. Example: "2024-12-31" ends retrieval at December 31st, 2024. Each data point represents the sentiment recorded at 12am UTC on that date.

response_example.datetime: Date and time when the Fear and Greed Index value was recorded, in ISO 8601 format (UTC timezone). Daily readings are recorded at 12:00 AM UTC. This timestamp allows for precise tracking of sentiment changes over time. (String, Date-Time)
response_example.from_date: The start date of the requested data range in ISO format. Only present when a date range was specified in the request. Useful for confirming the actual data range returned. (String, Date)
response_example.to_date: The end date of the requested data range in ISO format. Only present when a date range was specified in the request. Useful for confirming the actual data range returned. (String, Date)
response_example.value: Fear and Greed Index value ranging from 0 to 100. Lower values indicate market fear, higher values indicate greed. 0-19: Extreme Fear (market oversold, potential buying opportunity), 20-39: Fear (cautious sentiment), 40-59: Neutral (balanced market), 60-79: Greed (optimistic sentiment, consider profit-taking), 80-100: Extreme Greed (market overheated, potential correction ahead). (Number, Integer)
response_example.value_classification: Human-readable interpretation of the index value helping to quickly understand market sentiment. Possible values: "Extreme Fear" (1-19, investors overselling irrationally), "Fear" (20-39, cautious market sentiment), "Neutral" (40-59, balanced market conditions), "Greed" (60-79, optimistic investor sentiment), "Extreme Greed" (80-100, market likely overvalued). (String, Enumeration)

sample_payloads.api_name: CoinGecko NFTs Collection Market Chart API
sample_payloads.description:
✅ ACTUAL DATA - NFT collection historical time-series data (non-fungible tokens). Get historical time-series data for any NFT collection. Returns floor price, market cap, and trading volume over time. Returns HISTORICAL TIME-SERIES DATA for NFT COLLECTIONS (non-fungible tokens), NOT current snapshot data, NOT cryptocurrency OHLC data, or equity/stock data. Use this for trend analysis, price history tracking, and identifying patterns over different time periods. Each data point includes timestamp, floor price (USD and native currency), 24h volume (USD and native currency), and market cap (USD and native currency). Data granularity automatically adjusts: 1-14 days returns 5-minute intervals, 15+ days returns daily data. Maximum 1000 data points in response (full dataset stored in Redis). Accepts natural language collection names with fuzzy matching.

📊 RETURNS: Historical time-series data points (timestamp, floor price in USD and native currency, 24h volume in USD and native currency, market cap in USD and native currency) showing how NFT collection floor price, market cap, and trading volume have changed over specified time period. Data granularity: 5-minute intervals for 1-14 days, daily data for 15+ days.

✅ USE FOR:
- Analyze floor price trends over time
- Track volume changes across days/weeks/months
- Compare market cap evolution
- Identify price patterns for technical analysis
- Get historical data for any time period (1 day to maximum history)
- Calculate price changes and volatility
- NFT collection historical performance analysis
- Time-series chart data for visualization

❌ NOT FOR: Current NFT collection snapshot data (Use nft_collection_data) or NFT marketplace price comparison (Use nft_collection_tickers) or Cryptocurrency OHLC candlestick data (Use get_crypto_historical_price_data) or Global cryptocurrency market cap historical data (Use market_cap_chart) or Equity/stock historical data (Use equity market tools).

PARAMETERS:
- name: REQUIRED. NFT collection name in natural language. Supports fuzzy matching. Examples: 'Pudgy Penguins', 'pudgy penguin', 'bored ape', 'azuki', 'cryptopunks', 'mutant ape'. System automatically matches to correct collection. Case-insensitive.
- days: OPTIONAL. Time period for historical data (default: '1'). Use integers like '7', '30', '90', '365' or 'max' for all available history.

ℹ️ NOTE: This tool provides HISTORICAL TIME-SERIES DATA for NFT COLLECTIONS (non-fungible tokens), NOT cryptocurrencies. For current NFT collection snapshot data (floor price, market cap, holder stats), use nft_collection_data. For NFT marketplace price comparison across OpenSea, Blur, Magic Eden, etc., use nft_collection_tickers. For cryptocurrency OHLC candlestick data, use get_crypto_historical_price_data. For global cryptocurrency market cap historical data, use market_cap_chart. Data granularity adjusts automatically (5-minute intervals for 1-14 days, daily for 15+ days). Maximum 1000 data points in response (full dataset stored in Redis). This tool is the PRIMARY source for historical NFT collection time-series data.
sample_payloads.name: nft_collection_chart

optional.days: Lookback window for historical chart data (default: '1'). Accepts positive integers ('1' for 1 day, '7' for 1 week, '30' for 1 month, '90' for 3 months, '180' for 6 months, '365' for 1 year) or 'max' for maximum available history. Granularity adjusts automatically: 1-14 days provide 5-minutely data points, 15+ days provide daily data points (00:00 UTC). Invalid values default to '1'.

required.name: NFT collection name in natural language (required). Supports fuzzy matching. Examples: 'Pudgy Penguins', 'pudgy penguin', 'bored ape', 'azuki', 'cryptopunks', 'mutant ape'. System automatically matches to correct collection. Case-insensitive.

response_example.columns: List of column names in the dataset. (Array)
response_example.data: Time-series records with floor prices, volumes, and market cap. 652, "floor_price_native": 10.248, "h24_volume_usd": 961627.972, "h24_volume_native": 242.123, "market_cap_usd": 363757834.176}]. Limited to 1000 records in response, full data in Redis. (Array)
response_example.data_stats: Statistical summary including mean, min, max, standard deviation for numeric fields. 67, "floor_price_usd_min": 39800.00, "floor_price_usd_max": 43200.00}. (Object)
response_example.dataframe_shape: Dimensions of the DataFrame as [rows, columns]. (Array)
response_example.datetime: ISO 8601 formatted datetime in UTC for each data point. (String, Date)
response_example.days: Lookback window used for the query. (String)
response_example.floor_price_native: Minimum listing price in native blockchain currency at the timestamp. Number)
response_example.floor_price_usd: Minimum listing price in USD at the timestamp. Number)
response_example.h24_volume_native: 24-hour trading volume in native currency at the timestamp. Number)
response_example.h24_volume_usd: 24-hour trading volume in USD at the timestamp. Number)
response_example.market_cap_native: Total market capitalization in native currency at the timestamp. Number)
response_example.market_cap_usd: Total market capitalization in USD at the timestamp. Number)
response_example.redis_key: Redis storage key for full dataset access. (String)
response_example.stored_at: Timestamp when data was fetched and stored. (String, Date)
response_example.timestamp_ms: Unix timestamp in milliseconds for each data point. (Number)
response_example.total_data_points: Total number of data points available in full dataset. (Number)

sample_payloads.description: Generates formal account statements for specified time periods. Displays opening balance, closing balance, total deposits, total withdrawals, interest earned, fees charged, number of transactions, and net change. Supports credit card statements via account_type=credit_card and can include statement line items when available.
sample_payloads.description_ar:
ينشئ كشوفات حساب رسمية لفترات زمنية محددة. يعرض الرصيد الافتتاحي، الرصيد الختامي، إجمالي الإيداعات، إجمالي السحوبات، الفوائد المكتسبة، الرسوم المحصلة، عدد المعاملات، والتغيير الصافي. ينتج كشوفات شهرية، ربع سنوية، سنوية، أو فترات مخصصة مناسبة للسجلات والوثائق المالية.

استخدم عندما:
- كشوفات حساب رسمية (كشف الحساب، بيان الحساب)
- ملخصات فترية (كشف شهري، كشف ربع سنوي، كشف سنوي)
- تحليل الرصيد والمعاملات (الرصيد الافتتاحي، الرصيد الختامي، عدد المعاملات)
- السجلات المالية (الإيداعات، السحوبات، الفوائد، الرسوم)

كلمات مفتاحية: كشف الحساب، بيان الحساب، كشف شهري، كشف سنوي، رصيد افتتاحي، رصيد ختامي، معاملات الحساب
sample_payloads.name: statements

optional.account_id: Specific account ID to generate statement for (default: all user accounts). Filters statement to single account.
optional.account_type: Account type for specialized statements. Use 'credit_card' for card statements.
optional.end_date: End date for custom period statement in YYYY-MM-DD format. Only used when period='custom'. Defines the end of the statement period.
optional.include_statement_details: Include statement line items when available (default: false).
optional.period: Statement period (default: 'last_month'). Options: 'current_month' (month to date), 'last_month' (previous month), 'last_3_months' (last 3 months), 'last_6_months' (last 6 months), 'ytd' (year to date), 'custom' (requires start_date and end_date).
optional.start_date: Start date for custom period statement in YYYY-MM-DD format. Only used when period='custom'. Defines the beginning of the statement period.

response_example.account_statements: Account statements array. Each statement includes: account_id (integer), account_name (localized string), account_type (string - 'checking'/'savings'/'credit_card'/'brokerage_cash'), currency (string - 'SAR'), opened_at (date - account opening date), closed_at (date or null - account closing date), opening_balance (decimal - balance at period start), closing_balance (decimal - balance at period end), total_credits (decimal - sum of all deposits/credits in period), total_debits (decimal - sum of all withdrawals/debits in period), interest_earned (decimal - interest accrued in period), fees_charged (decimal - fees charged in period), days_in_period (integer - number of days covered), transaction_count (integer - total number of transactions in period), net_change (decimal - calculated as total_credits minus total_debits). Example: [{account_id: 12345, account_name: 'My Checking Account', account_type: 'checking', currency: 'SAR', opened_at: '2023-01-15', closed_at: null, opening_balance: 15000.00, closing_balance: 18500.00, total_credits: 12000.00, total_debits: 8500.00, interest_earned: 0.00, fees_charged: 0.00, days_in_period: 31, transaction_count: 45, net_change: 3500.00}]. (Array of Objects)
response_example.card_statements: Optional credit card statements array when account_type=credit_card. Each statement includes: statement_id, card_id, statement_date, due_date, opening_balance, closing_balance, total_purchases, total_payments, total_fees, total_interest, minimum_payment_due, payment_status, paid_amount, paid_date, card_number, card_type. (Array of Objects)
response_example.statement_line_items: Optional line items array when include_statement_details is true and line items are available. (Array of Objects)
response_example.statement_period: Statement period in ISO format. Example: '2024-10-01 to 2024-10-31'. Shows the exact date range covered by the statement. (String)
response_example.statement_period_display: Human-readable statement period. Example: 'October 2024' for single month. Formatted based on language preference. (String)

sample_payloads.description: Provides information needed to prepare money transfers. Shows available source accounts with balances, saved beneficiaries with account details, standing orders, recent transfer history, and daily and monthly transfer limits. Supports managing beneficiaries and standing orders.
sample_payloads.description_ar:
يوفر المعلومات المطلوبة لتحضير تحويلات الأموال. يوضح الحسابات المصدرية المتاحة مع الأرصدة، المستفيدين المحفوظين مع تفاصيل الحسابات، سجل التحويلات الأخيرة، وحدود التحويل اليومية والشهرية. يبحث عن المستفيدين بالاسم للعثور على المستلمين للتحويلات.

استخدم عندما:
- تحضير تحويلات الأموال (معلومات للتحويل)
- الحسابات المصدرية (الحسابات المتاحة والأرصدة)
- المستفيدين المحفوظين (البحث عن المستفيدين)
- حدود وسجل التحويلات

كلمات مفتاحية: تحويل أموال، حسابات مصدرية، مستفيدين، حدود تحويل، سجل تحويلات
sample_payloads.name: transfer_money

optional.amount: Transfer amount in source account currency. Must be positive number. Example: 1000.00
optional.beneficiary_id: Saved beneficiary unique identifier for external transfers. Mutually exclusive with to_account_id.
optional.beneficiary_name: Beneficiary name for fuzzy search. Example: 'Nesma Hold' matches 'Nesma Holding'.
optional.from_account_id: Source account identifier for the transfer (default: user's primary account).
optional.history_days: Number of days of transfer history to include (default: 30, max: 365). Valid range: 1-365.
optional.include_history: Include recent transfer history in response (default: true).
optional.include_standing_orders: Include standing orders list in response (default: false).
optional.purpose: Transfer purpose or reason. Required for compliance, especially for international transfers.
optional.to_account_id: Destination account identifier for internal transfers. Mutually exclusive with beneficiary_id.

response_example.beneficiaries: Saved beneficiaries list. Each beneficiary includes: beneficiary_id, beneficiary_name, account_number, bank_name, swift_code (for international), beneficiary_type, and last_used_date. (Array of Objects)
response_example.beneficiary_action_result: Optional beneficiary action result containing status and beneficiary details. (Object)
response_example.source_accounts: Available source accounts array. Each account includes: account_id, account_name, account_type, current_balance, available_balance, currency, daily_transfer_limit, and monthly_transfer_limit. (Array of Objects)
response_example.standing_order_action_result: Optional standing order action result containing status and standing order details. (Object)
response_example.standing_orders: Optional standing orders list. Each entry includes: standing_order_id, from_account_id, to_account_id/beneficiary_id, amount, currency, frequency, next_execution_date, status. (Array of Objects)
response_example.summary: Summary information array with transfer statistics and insights. (Array of Objects)
response_example.transfer_history: Recent transfer history array. Each transfer includes: transfer_id, date, amount, currency, from_account, to_account/beneficiary, transfer_type, status, and reference_number. (Array of Objects)
response_example.transfer_limits: Transfer limits information object. Includes: daily_limit and monthly_limit labels for display. (Object)

acceptable_parameter_mixes.description: Default - Top 10 categories for current month
Top 10 categories for specific date range
Top 10 categories with minimum 100 SAR transactions
Top 20 categories for specific period, filtering small transactions

sample_payloads.description: Ranks spending categories by total amount spent from highest to lowest. Returns top spending categories with total spent, transaction count, percentage of total expenses, average transaction size, and days with spending. Provides overall spending summary including total transactions, total expenses, daily averages, and spending frequency for the specified period.
sample_payloads.description_ar:
يرتب فئات الإنفاق حسب إجمالي المبلغ المنفق من الأعلى إلى الأدنى. يرجع أكبر فئات الإنفاق مع إجمالي المنفق، عدد المعاملات، النسبة المئوية من إجمالي المصروفات، متوسط حجم المعاملة، وأيام الإنفاق. يوفر ملخص إنفاق شامل يشمل إجمالي المعاملات، إجمالي المصروفات، متوسطات يومية، وتكرار الإنفاق للفترة المحددة.

استخدم عندما:
- ترتيب فئات الإنفاق (أكبر فئات الإنفاق، ترتيب المصروفات)
- تحليل المصروفات (إجمالي المنفق، عدد المعاملات، نسب المئوية)
- متوسطات وحسابات (متوسط المعاملات، متوسط يومي، تكرار الإنفاق)
- ملخص الإنفاق الشامل (إجمالي المصروفات، تحليل الفترة)

كلمات مفتاحية: أكبر المصروفات، ترتيب الإنفاق، فئات الإنفاق، إحصائيات المصروفات، متوسط الإنفاق
sample_payloads.name: top_expenses

optional.end_date: End date for analysis period in YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults to today if not provided. Must be greater than or equal to start_date. Example: '2024-01-31'
optional.limit: Number of top spending categories to return in ranked order. Integer between 1 and 50. Default: 10. Higher limit shows more categories but may include less significant expenses.
optional.min_amount: Minimum transaction amount in SAR to include in analysis. Non-negative float. Default: 0. Use to filter out small transactions and focus on significant expenses. Example: 100.0 to exclude transactions under 100 SAR.
optional.start_date: Start date for analysis period in YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults to first day of current month if not provided. Example: '2024-01-01'

response_example.overall_summary: Comprehensive spending summary containing: total_transactions (count of all transactions), total_expenses (sum in SAR), average_transaction (mean amount), min_transaction (smallest), max_transaction (largest), daily_average (expenses per day), categories_used (distinct categories count), merchants_visited (unique merchant count), days_with_spending (days with at least one transaction), spending_frequency (percentage of days with spending), time_period (e.g. 'Current Month'), start_date, end_date. (Object with numeric and string fields)
response_example.top_expenses: Array of top spending categories ranked by total_spent (highest first). Each category object includes: rank (1-based position), category (category name), total_spent (sum in SAR), transaction_count (number of transactions), percentage_of_total (percent of all expenses), average_transaction (mean transaction amount), largest_transaction (biggest single transaction), smallest_transaction (smallest single transaction), days_with_spending (days with spending in this category). (Array of Objects, length up to limit parameter)

sample_payloads.description: Analyzes temporal spending patterns showing when spending occurs. Calculates daily average spending, identifies peak spending days and times, and shows spending distribution across days of week and times of day. Displays spending trends with direction and volatility measurements. Detects unusual spending spikes and behavioral patterns in spending habits.
sample_payloads.description_ar:
يحلل أنماط الإنفاق الزمنية موضحاً متى يحدث الإنفاق. يحسب متوسط الإنفاق اليومي، يحدد أيام وأوقات الذروة في الإنفاق، ويوضح توزيع الإنفاق عبر أيام الأسبوع وأوقات اليوم. يعرض اتجاهات الإنفاق مع قياسات الاتجاه والتقلبات. يكتشف الارتفاعات غير العادية في الإنفاق وأنماط السلوك في عادات الإنفاق.

استخدم عندما:
- تحليل الأنماط الزمنية للإنفاق (متى يحدث الإنفاق)
- حساب متوسط الإنفاق اليومي وتحديد أوقات الذروة
- توزيع الإنفاق عبر أيام الأسبوع وأوقات اليوم
- عرض اتجاهات الإنفاق مع الاتجاه والتقلبات
- اكتشاف الارتفاعات غير العادية وأنماط السلوك

كلمات مفتاحية: أنماط الإنفاق الزمنية، متوسط يومي، أوقات الذروة، توزيع أسبوعي، اتجاهات الإنفاق، ارتفاعات غير عادية
sample_payloads.name: spending_trend

optional.end_date: End date for trend analysis in YYYY-MM-DD format. ONLY provide if user explicitly mentions a specific date. If omitted, automatically defaults to today.
optional.start_date: Start date for trend analysis in YYYY-MM-DD format. ONLY provide if user explicitly mentions a specific date. If omitted, automatically defaults to 6 months ago.

response_example.analysis_period: The date range that was analyzed in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD'. (String)
response_example.behavioral_insights: Array of behavioral insights. Each object contains: type, title, description, and severity. (Array of Objects)
response_example.currency: Currency code used in the analysis (e.g., 'SAR'). (String)
response_example.monthly_pattern: Array of month-by-month spending data. Each object contains: month, month_name, total_spent, transaction_count, avg_transaction, and status. (Array of Objects)
response_example.peak_spending: Object containing peak spending information with: day, day_amount, time, and time_amount. (Object)
response_example.period_description: Automatically determined period description such as 'Last 6 Months', 'Last Month', 'Last Week', or 'Custom Period'. (String)
response_example.spending_spikes: Array of top 5 unusual spending days. Each object contains: date, amount, and percentage_above_average. (Array of Objects)
response_example.summary: Overall spending summary object containing: total_spending, daily_average, total_transactions, days_analyzed, trend_direction, trend_percentage, and volatility. (Object)
response_example.time_of_day_pattern: Array of spending data by time period (Morning/Afternoon/Evening/Night). Each object contains: period, period_en, total_spent, transaction_count, and avg_transaction. (Array of Objects)
response_example.weekly_pattern: Array of spending data for each day of the week. Each object contains: day, day_en, total_spent, transaction_count, avg_transaction, and status. (Array of Objects)

sample_payloads.description: Analyzes spending patterns across categories or searches for individual transactions. Shows comprehensive breakdown of all spending categories with total amounts, percentages, and transaction counts. Can filter by category, date range, merchant name, or transaction amount. Lists individual transactions with dates, merchants, amounts, and categories. Provides spending totals, averages, and insights.
sample_payloads.description_ar:
يحلل أنماط الإنفاق عبر الفئات أو يبحث عن المعاملات الفردية. يوضح تفصيلاً شاملاً لجميع فئات الإنفاق مع المبالغ الإجمالية، النسب المئوية، وعدد المعاملات. يمكن التصفية حسب الفئة، النطاق الزمني، اسم التاجر، أو مبلغ المعاملة. يسرد المعاملات الفردية مع التواريخ، التجار، المبالغ، والفئات. يوفر إجماليات الإنفاق، المعدلات، والرؤى.

استخدم عندما:
- تحليل أنماط الإنفاق عبر الفئات
- البحث عن معاملات فردية
- تفصيل شامل لفئات الإنفاق (مبالغ، نسب، عدد)
- تصفية حسب فئة أو تاجر أو مبلغ
- عرض المعاملات مع التفاصيل الكاملة
- إجماليات ومعدلات الإنفاق

كلمات مفتاحية: تحليل الإنفاق، أنماط الإنفاق، فئات الإنفاق، معاملات فردية، بحث معاملات، إجماليات إنفاق
sample_payloads.name: spending_lookup

optional.all_time: Analyze all available transaction history (default: false). When true, analyzes entire spending history. When false, defaults to last month analysis.
optional.budget_targets: Include budget targets vs actual spending comparison (default: false).
optional.category_filter: Filter analysis to specific spending category. Options: 'groceries', 'dining', 'transportation', 'shopping', 'entertainment', etc.
optional.currency: Currency code for analysis and reporting (default: account's primary currency). Supports SAR, USD, EUR, GBP, AED.
optional.end_date: End date for custom period analysis in YYYY-MM-DD format (default: last day of last month). Ignored if all_time=true.
optional.include_breakdowns: Include category and merchant breakdowns in search results (default: true). Shows distribution across categories and top merchants.
optional.include_forecasting: Include spending forecast for remainder of period (default: false).
optional.include_summary: Include search summary statistics with total amount, count, and averages (default: true). Provides aggregate data for search results.
optional.limit: Maximum number of transaction results to return (default: 50, max: 500). Controls pagination of search results.
optional.list_transactions: Set to True when user asks to list/show/display transactions (e.g., 'show me my recent transactions', 'list my transactions', 'display my purchases'). TRIGGERS SEARCH MODE. When true, returns list of individual transactions with full details instead of spending analysis.
optional.max_amount: Maximum transaction amount filter (default: no maximum). Only returns transactions with amounts less than or equal to this value. TRIGGERS SEARCH MODE.
optional.merchant_category: Category filter for merchant types. Examples: 'groceries', 'dining', 'transportation', 'shopping', 'entertainment', etc. Used to narrow down transaction searches.
optional.merchant_name: Merchant name to search for (supports fuzzy matching for typos and variations). Extract merchant names from queries like 'at Tim Hortons', 'from Amazon', 'to Mobily'. Examples: Tim Hortons, Al Baik, Mobily, Amazon, Starbucks, etc. TRIGGERS SEARCH MODE.
optional.merchant_subcategory: Subcategory filter for merchant types. Examples: 'fast_food', 'fine_dining', 'bakery', 'desserts', 'fresh_produce', 'cafe', 'supermarket', etc. Used to narrow down transaction searches.
optional.min_amount: Minimum transaction amount filter (default: no minimum). Only returns transactions with amounts greater than or equal to this value. TRIGGERS SEARCH MODE.
optional.multi_month_analysis: Enable multi-month comparison analysis (default: false). Automatically set to true when date range spans multiple months.
optional.sort_by: Sort order for transaction results. Options: 'date_desc' (newest first), 'date_asc' (oldest first), 'amount_desc' (highest first), 'amount_asc' (lowest first). Default: 'date_desc'.
optional.start_date: Start date for custom period analysis in YYYY-MM-DD format (default: first day of last month). Ignored if all_time=true.

response_example.analysis_period: The period being analyzed. Example: 'September 2024' for single month or 'July 2024 - September 2024' for multi-month. (String)
response_example.budget_performance: Budget vs actual comparison when budget targets are available. Shows target amounts, actual spending, variance, and adherence percentage by category. Null if no budget targets set. (Object or Null)
response_example.category_filter: Applied category filter if any. Example: 'groceries' for category-specific analysis. Null if analyzing all categories. (String or Null)
response_example.category_transactions: Detailed transaction list for filtered category. Only present when category_filter is specified. Includes transaction details, amounts, dates, merchants. Null if no category filter. (Array of Objects or Null)
response_example.data_available: Boolean indicating if meaningful data is available for analysis. False if no transactions in period. (Boolean)
response_example.expense_summary: Total expenses breakdown by categories. Includes all spending categories with amounts, percentages, and transaction counts. Null if no expense data. (Object or Null)
response_example.income_summary: Total income breakdown by sources. Includes salary, transfers, interest, and other income categories with amounts and percentages. Null if no income data available. (Object or Null)
response_example.insights: Financial insights and recommendations array. AI-generated insights about spending patterns, unusual transactions, savings opportunities, and budget recommendations. Null if insufficient data. (Array of Objects or Null)
response_example.multi_month_analysis: Boolean indicating if analysis spans multiple months. Affects response structure and comparison data. (Boolean)
response_example.spending_categories: Detailed spending by category array. Each category includes name, total amount, percentage of total spending, transaction count, and average transaction size. Sorted by amount descending. Null if no data. (Array of Objects or Null)
response_example.transaction_trends: Daily or weekly spending patterns showing trends over time. Includes date, amount, and transaction count for each period. Null if insufficient data. (Array of Objects or Null)

استخدم عندما:
- دفع فواتير (دفع فاتورة، تسديد فاتورة)
- البحث عن فواتير قابلة للدفع (فواتير مستحقة، فواتير معلقة)
- معاينة الدفع (مراجعة الدفع قبل التنفيذ)
- تنفيذ معاملات الدفع (تأكيد الدفع، إتمام الدفع)

sample_payloads.name: pay_bill

optional.bill_category: Bill category to lookup and pay (OPTIONAL). Alternative to provider_name. Use when user says 'Pay my utilities bill' or 'Pay bills for telecom'. Tool will find matching bills by category using fuzzy matching. Supports both English and Arabic category names. Examples: 'utilities', 'telecom', 'subscription', 'insurance'. If both bill_category and provider_name are provided, bill_category takes precedence.
optional.bill_id: Specific bill ID to pay. Use when user explicitly provides a bill ID number like 'pay bill 5' or 'pay bill ID 123'. If not provided, use provider_name to lookup bills.
optional.end_due_date: End of due date range (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with start_due_date for filtering bills. For 'the February one' set to '2026-02-28'. For exact date set both start and end to same value. For Q1 set to '2026-03-31'.
optional.payment_amount: Custom payment amount in SAR. If not provided, pays the full amount due on the bill.
optional.payment_confirmation: Boolean flag to execute payment (default: false for preview). Set to true when user confirms payment with phrases like 'yes', 'confirm', 'proceed', 'pay now'.
optional.provider_name: Provider name OR bill category for automatic bill lookup. Supports fuzzy matching on both categories and provider names, works with both English and Arabic. Examples: 'Saudi Gas', 'DEWA', 'Etisalat', 'utilities', 'telecom', 'subscription', 'Shahid', 'STC', 'Netflix', 'موبايلي', 'شركة الكهرباء'. Tool tries category matching first, then falls back to provider name matching.
optional.source_account_id: Account ID to pay from (default: '1' - user's primary account). User can specify 'from savings' or 'from account 2'.
optional.start_due_date: Start of due date range (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with end_due_date for filtering bills. For 'the February one' set to '2026-02-01'. For exact date like 'due on Jan 12' set both start and end to '2026-01-12'. For Q1 set to '2026-01-01'.

response_example.available_accounts: Array of available accounts for payment. Each account includes: account_id, account_name, current_balance. (Array of Objects)
response_example.bill_details: Bill details object containing: bill_id, provider_name, bill_category, amount_due, due_date. (Object)
response_example.confirmation_required: Set to true when multiple bills match the provider_name. Response includes bill_options array for user to choose from. (Boolean)
response_example.next_steps: Array of next steps for the user. Each step includes: step_id, step_name, step_description. (Array of Objects or Null)
response_example.payment_preview: Payment preview object containing: preview_available (boolean), bill_details (object with bill_id, provider_name, bill_category, amount_due, due_date), payment_amount (decimal), source_account (object with account_id, account_name, current_balance), new_account_balance (decimal after payment), confirmation_required (boolean), confirmation_message (string). (Object or Null)
response_example.payment_result: Payment result object containing: success (boolean), transaction_id (string), payment_date (date), amount_paid (decimal), source_account (string), provider (string), bill_category (string), new_account_balance (decimal), bill_details (updated bill object with is_paid=true). (Object or Null)

sample_payloads.description:
INCOME GROWTH & TREND ANALYSIS: Tracks and analyzes income growth over time from 2019 to present day, showing trends, growth rates, year-over-year comparisons, income trajectory, and salary progression. Analyzes historical income performance with comprehensive growth metrics. Returns different widget structures based on grouping parameter.

WIDGET VARIANTS:
1. MONTHLY (default): widget_id='income_trend_widget_monthly' - Shows current income, change vs last month (SAR), growth rate (%), year-over-year change (%), and 7-month trend values
2. YEARLY: widget_id='income_trend_widget_yearly' - Shows annual income (2025), YoY growth (%), growth vs last year (SAR), CAGR (7 years), and 7-year trajectory (2019-2025)

USE THIS WHEN:
- User asks about income TRENDS and GROWTH patterns
- User wants to see HOW income has changed over time
- User requests year-over-year income comparisons
- User asks about salary progression or income trajectory
- User wants CAGR or long-term growth analysis

DO NOT USE FOR:
- Current income queries (use account_summary)
- Listing income transactions (use search_transactions)
- Income source tracking (use search_transactions)

ANALYSIS PERIOD: Always analyzes income from 2019 to present day (no custom date ranges)

Parameters Usage:
- grouping: OPTIONAL. Time grouping: 'monthly' (default) or 'yearly'. Determines widget structure and metrics returned.
- include_bonuses: OPTIONAL. Include bonus income in analysis (default: true). When false, excludes bonuses and shows only regular income.
- account_id: OPTIONAL. Specific account to analyze (default: all accounts). When provided, analyzes only that account's income.

sample_payloads.description:
🔬 CRYPTO PROJECT FUNDAMENTALS (WORKFLOW TOOL) - Get the complete fundamental picture of any cryptocurrency project including what it does, who's building it, development activity on GitHub (commits, stars, forks, issues, pull requests, code additions/deletions), market positioning, supply details, and price performance. See the project's description, categories, platforms, social presence, and how actively the team is shipping code. Returns structured LLM-friendly analysis focusing on PROJECT FUNDAMENTALS and DEVELOPMENT ACTIVITY (NOT just market analysis, NOT raw metadata). This is a WORKFLOW TOOL that aggregates project data from CoinGecko to deliver comprehensive fundamental analysis.

📊 RETURNS:
- Timestamp: Analysis timestamp in ISO format
- Description: Analysis description and data source (CoinGecko API)
- Units explanation: Units for price (USD), market cap (USD), volume (USD), changes (percentage), supply (tokens), stars (GitHub stars), forks (GitHub forks)
- Symbol: Cryptocurrency symbol analyzed
- Basic info: Essential project information including name (full cryptocurrency name), symbol (trading symbol), rank (market capitalization rank), current price USD, market cap USD, volume 24h USD, ATH (all-time high price), ATL (all-time low price), circulating supply (current circulating tokens), total supply (total tokens created), max supply (maximum possible supply or unlimited)
- Project details: Comprehensive project information including website (official project website URL), GitHub (GitHub repository URL), Twitter (official Twitter handle), Telegram (Telegram channel identifier), Reddit (Reddit community URL), categories (project categories and classifications: DeFi, Layer 1, Gaming, etc.), platforms (blockchain platforms where token exists: Ethereum, Binance Smart Chain, etc.), genesis date (date when cryptocurrency was created), hashing algorithm (hashing algorithm used), consensus algorithm (consensus mechanism: PoW, PoS, etc.), proof type (proof mechanism type), description (project description truncated to 500 characters)
- Developer activity: GitHub metrics showing development progress including GitHub stars (number of GitHub stars for project), GitHub forks (number of GitHub forks), total issues (total number of GitHub issues), closed issues (number of closed GitHub issues), pull requests merged (number of merged pull requests), pull request contributors (number of contributors to pull requests), code additions (lines of code added in last 4 weeks), code deletions (lines of code deleted in last 4 weeks), commit count 4 weeks (number of commits in last 4 weeks), and development activity assessment (how actively team is shipping code)
- Market data: Price, supply, market cap, and performance data including price changes (1h, 24h, 7d, 30d, 200d, 1y percentage changes), market cap rank (current market capitalization rank), fully diluted valuation (fully diluted market valuation), total value locked (total value locked in DeFi protocols if applicable), high 24h (24-hour high price), low 24h (24-hour low price), and additional market metrics

✅ USE FOR:
- Getting complete fundamental picture of cryptocurrency project (project fundamentals analysis)
- Understanding what the project does (description, categories, platforms)
- Researching who's building it (developer activity, GitHub metrics)
- Analyzing development activity on GitHub (commits, stars, forks, issues, PRs, code changes)
- Seeing how actively team is shipping code (4-week commit count, code additions/deletions)
- Understanding project's description, categories, and platforms
- Researching social presence (Twitter, Telegram, Reddit links)
- Analyzing market positioning (market cap rank, price performance)
- Researching supply details (circulating, total, max supply)
- Getting comprehensive fundamental analysis in one structured response
- Due diligence on cryptocurrency projects (fundamentals research)

❌ NOT FOR:
- Market-focused analysis without fundamentals (Use crypto_deep_dive workflow tool instead - focuses on price performance, market position, trading activity)
- Raw metadata without structured analysis (Use cryptocurrency_info_lookup instead - returns raw data structure, requires TraderVerse ID format)
- Historical price performance statistics (Use price_performance_stats_lookup instead - OHLC summary per time period)
- OHLC candlestick time-series data (Use get_crypto_historical_price_data instead - continuous candlestick series)
- Individual cryptocurrency price data only (Use get_crypto_historical_price_data instead - OHLC data)
- Simple price/volume analysis (Use crypto_deep_dive instead - market-focused analysis)

ℹ️ NOTE: 1) WORKFLOW TOOL: This is a workflow tool that aggregates project data from CoinGecko API (coins/{coin_id} endpoint with developer_data, community_data, market_data flags) to provide structured LLM-friendly analysis. Returns JSON format with basic_info, project_details, developer_activity, market_data, timestamp, description, units, and symbol sections. 2) FUNDAMENTALS FOCUS: Focuses on PROJECT FUNDAMENTALS (what it does, who's building it, GitHub activity, project details, social presence), NOT just market analysis. For market-focused analysis (price performance, market position, trading activity), use crypto_deep_dive. 3) GITHUB ACTIVITY: Provides detailed GitHub development metrics including stars, forks, issues, pull requests, commits (last 4 weeks), code additions/deletions (last 4 weeks), and contributor counts. Shows how actively team is shipping code. 4) PROJECT DETAILS: Includes comprehensive project information: description, categories, platforms, genesis date, hashing algorithm, consensus algorithm, proof type, and social media links (website, GitHub, Twitter, Telegram, Reddit). 5) SIMPLE SYMBOL FORMAT: Uses simple cryptocurrency symbols (BTC, ETH, ADA), NOT TraderVerse ID format. Symbol is automatically mapped to CoinGecko coin ID internally. 6) STRUCTURED ANALYSIS: Returns LLM-friendly structured analysis with interpreted metrics and context, NOT raw data structure. For raw metadata, use cryptocurrency_info_lookup (requires TraderVerse ID format like 'BTC_CRYPTO'). 7) MARKET DATA: Includes current market snapshot (price, market cap, volume, ATH/ATL) and price performance (1h, 24h, 7d, 30d, 200d, 1y changes) but focuses on fundamentals context, not deep market analysis. 8) SUPPLY DETAILS: Provides circulating supply, total supply, and max supply information for token economics analysis. 9) PARAMETERS: symbol (REQUIRED). Cryptocurrency symbol in simple format: 'BTC', 'ETH', 'ADA', 'SOL', etc. (NOT TraderVerse ID format). Symbol is automatically mapped to CoinGecko coin ID. 10) KEY DISTINCTION: This tool provides FUNDAMENTALS-FOCUSED ANALYSIS (project details, GitHub activity, development metrics, social presence), while crypto_deep_dive provides MARKET-FOCUSED ANALYSIS (price performance, market position, trading activity, supply metrics). Both use simple symbol format and return structured analysis, but focus on different aspects. Perfect for researching project fundamentals, development activity, and team shipping velocity.
sample_payloads.name: crypto_project_fundamentals

response_example.market_fundamentals: Price, supply, market cap, and performance data. (Object)
response_example.project_identity: Official links, social presence, and project description. (Object)
response_example.project_overview: What the project is and its market position. (Object)

sample_payloads.description:
🔥 DEX TRENDING POOLS (ALL NETWORKS) - Find trending liquidity pools across ALL blockchain networks. Returns HOTTEST DEX POOLS based on recent trading activity, volume, and user engagement across Ethereum, Solana, Base, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, and more, NOT pools on a specific network, top pools by volume/transactions, pools on a specific DEX, centralized exchange volume, or on-chain token trades.

📊 RETURNS: Trending DEX liquidity pool data including pool address (contract address), token pair info (base_token and quote_token symbols, addresses, prices), 24h volume (trading volume in USD over 24 hours), transaction count (number of transactions in 24 hours), TVL (total value locked in USD), price changes across multiple timeframes (5m, 1h, 6h, 24h price change percentages), buy/sell ratios (buy vs sell transaction counts), network details (blockchain network ID: eth, solana, base, polygon, arbitrum, etc.), DEX platform info (uniswap_v3, pancakeswap, raydium, etc.), fee tier (pool fee percentage), reserve amounts (token reserves in USD), pools_redis_key (internal storage key for pools data), included_redis_key (internal storage key for included resources if include parameter used), pools_dataframe_shape (dimensions of pools dataset), pools_columns (names of all data fields), total_pools (total count of pools retrieved), total_pages_fetched (number of pages fetched), pools_data_stats (statistical summary of pools data), and stored_at (timestamp when data was fetched). Rankings determined by user engagement, market activity, liquidity, and security checks. Automatically fetches multiple pages (20 pools per page, default 5 pages = 100 pools). Data updates every 30 seconds from on-chain sources.

✅ USE FOR:
- Discovering trending pools across ALL blockchain networks (no network parameter needed)
- Finding hot trading pairs with surging volume across entire DeFi ecosystem
- Identifying emerging liquidity opportunities across all chains
- Tracking cross-chain DeFi momentum and market trends
- Researching viral tokens and new pairs across all networks
- Monitoring overall market sentiment across entire DeFi landscape
- Finding hottest pools based on recent activity (5m, 1h, 6h, 24h timeframes)
- Cross-chain liquidity trend analysis
- DeFi opportunity discovery across all major blockchains
- Market momentum tracking across Ethereum, Solana, Base, Polygon, Arbitrum, and more

❌ NOT FOR: Trending pools on a specific network (Use trending_pools_network instead, requires network parameter) or Top pools by volume/transactions on a network (Use top_pools_network instead) or Top pools on a specific DEX (Use top_pools_dex instead) or Centralized exchange volume data (Use exchange_volume_chart instead) or On-chain token trades (Use trades_by_token_address_lookup instead) or Comprehensive pool analysis with all series types (Use pools_list_lookup with series='trending_all' instead).

ℹ️ NOTE: 1) This tool finds TRENDING POOLS ACROSS ALL BLOCKCHAIN NETWORKS (no network parameter needed), NOT pools on a specific network or DEX. For trending pools on a specific network (Ethereum, Solana, Base, etc.), use trending_pools_network tool (requires network parameter). For top pools by volume/transactions on a network, use top_pools_network tool. For top pools on a specific DEX, use top_pools_dex tool. 2) DURATION PARAMETER: OPTIONAL (default: '24h'). Controls trending timeframe: '5m' for very recent activity (5-minute trends), '1h' for hourly trends, '6h' for 6-hour window, '24h' for daily trending. Shorter durations (5m, 1h) show most recent activity spikes. Longer durations (24h) show sustained trending patterns. 3) MAX_PAGES PARAMETER: OPTIONAL (default: 5 = 100 pools). Each page contains 20 pools. Set to 5 for quick overview (100 pools). Set to -1 to get all available pools (may take several minutes). Tool automatically fetches subsequent pages from starting page. 4) INCLUDE PARAMETER: OPTIONAL (default: 'base_token'). Additional data to include: 'base_token' for base token info (default), 'quote_token' for quote token info, 'dex' for DEX platform details, 'network' for blockchain network details. Comma-separated combinations allowed (e.g., 'base_token,quote_token,dex,network'). Included resources stored separately in Redis with included_redis_key. 5) PAGE PARAMETER: OPTIONAL (default: 1). Starting page number. Tool automatically fetches subsequent pages from this point up to max_pages limit. 6) DATA SOURCE: Real-time on-chain data from DEX smart contracts via CoinGecko Pro API. Pool metrics updated continuously. Trending calculations based on recent activity. 7) PERFECT FOR: Users who want to discover trending pools across the entire DeFi ecosystem without focusing on a specific network or DEX. For network-specific or DEX-specific analysis, use the corresponding specialized tools.

steps.instruction: In a Dutch oven, cook the beef over medium heat until it is no longer pink, breaking it into crumbles with a wooden spoon or spatula as it cooks. Drain some or all the excess fat. Add the taco seasoning to the beef and mix well.

Stir in the water, beans, corn, tomatoes and optional chiles. Stir in the ranch dressing mix. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat. Simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally for 15 minutes or until heated through.
Serve the soup. If desired, garnish bowls with sour cream and green onions.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees F.

Heat a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat. Add the vegetable oil. Sprinkle both sides of the chicken with some salt and pepper, the brown sugar, paprika and cayenne. Sear both sides of the chicken in the skillet until golden, about 4 minutes. Add in the wing sauce, bring to a simmer and finish off in the oven until the chicken registers 165 degrees F on an instant-read thermometer, about 15 minutes. Let cool, and then pull the meat with your hands or two forks. Adjust the seasoning if necessary, add the butter if using, and place back in the oven at BROIL until crusty and caramelized on top, about 4 minutes.
In a bowl, the combine carrots, celery and tomatoes, and season with salt and pepper. Mix until combined.
Cut the French loaf horizontally. Place an even layer of blue cheese crumbles on the bottom, then the chicken and then the veggies. Serve with a side of Homemade Ranch Dressing.
Mix the mayonnaise, buttermilk, garlic, dill, honey, salt, mustard and pepper in a bowl. Store in an airtight container.

sample_payloads.description:
ACTUAL DATA - Top market movers list (gainers, losers, most traded). Retrieve top market movers including biggest gainers, losers, and most actively traded stocks. Provides real-time data on stocks with the largest price movements and highest trading volumes. Returns a LIST of top-performing stocks (ranked by price change or volume), NOT individual stock price data or comprehensive market analysis. Focused tool for identifying market momentum leaders.

 RETURNS: Top gaining stocks (biggest price increases), top declining stocks (biggest price decreases), most actively traded stocks (highest volume), and combined movers list. Each entry includes symbol, price change, percentage change, volume, and market cap.

 USE FOR:
- Identifying biggest market gainers and losers
- Finding most actively traded stocks
- Market momentum and volatility tracking
- Quick market movers screening
- Real-time market activity monitoring

 NOT FOR: Comprehensive market analysis with sector/industry data or Market movers with news integration or Individual stock price data or Sector/industry performance.

PARAMETERS:
- type: OPTIONAL. Default: 'gainers'. Options: 'gainers' (top gaining stocks), 'losers' (top declining stocks), 'most_traded' (most actively traded stocks by volume), 'all_movers' (all of the above combined).

ℹ NOTE: This tool provides FOCUSED top movers list (gainers, losers, most traded), NOT comprehensive market analysis.
sample_payloads.name: get_top_movers

optional.type: Data type to retrieve. Options: 'gainers' (default) for top gaining stocks, 'losers' for top declining stocks, 'most_traded' for most actively traded stocks by volume, 'all_movers' for all of the above combined.

type.all_movers: Combined movers list showing comprehensive market momentum analysis across all categories (gainers, losers, most traded). Provides unified view of market activity including top performers, worst performers, and most active stocks. Used for comprehensive market momentum analysis and market-wide activity assessment.
type.gainers: Top gaining stocks showing stocks with the highest percentage price increases, ranked by changesPercentage in descending order. Includes symbol, name, price, change, changesPercentage, and exchange. Used for momentum trading strategies and identifying strong performers with significant upward price movements.
type.losers: Top declining stocks showing stocks with the highest percentage price decreases, ranked by changesPercentage in ascending order (most negative first). Includes symbol, name, price, change, changesPercentage, and exchange. Used for contrarian strategies and identifying oversold opportunities or stocks experiencing significant downward pressure.
type.most_traded: Most actively traded stocks showing stocks with the highest trading volume, ranked by volume in descending order. Includes symbol, name, price, change, changesPercentage, and exchange. Used for liquidity analysis and identifying high-activity stocks with significant trading interest.

response_example.change: Absolute price change from the previous close, expressed in USD (e.g., 5.50 for a $5.50 increase, -3.20 for a $3.20 decrease). Positive values indicate price increases, negative values indicate price decreases. Used for price movement analysis. (Number)
response_example.changesPercentage: Percentage change in stock price from the previous close, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 5.25 for +5.25% gain, -2.10 for -2.10% loss). Calculated as (current price - previous close) / previous close × 100. Positive values indicate gains, negative values indicate losses. Used for relative performance measurement and momentum analysis. (Number)
response_example.exchange: Stock exchange code where the stock is listed (e.g., 'NASDAQ', 'NYSE', 'AMEX'). Indicates the primary trading venue for the stock. Used for exchange-specific filtering and analysis. (String)
response_example.name: Full company name of the top mover (e.g., 'Apple Inc.', 'Tesla, Inc.', 'NVIDIA Corporation'). Provides official company identification beyond the ticker symbol. (String)
response_example.price: Current stock price per share, expressed in USD (e.g., 150.25 for $150.25 per share). Represents the latest trading price at the time of data retrieval. Used for price analysis and valuation. (Number)
response_example.symbol: Stock ticker symbol of the top mover (e.g., 'AAPL', 'TSLA', 'NVDA'). Identifies the stock with significant price movement or trading volume. Used for symbol-level analysis and identification. (String)

sample_payloads.description:
ESTIMATES DATA - Analyst price predictions for future stock prices. A powerful tool to retrieve and analyze analyst price target data for public companies. Returns forward-looking price predictions (where analysts think the stock price will go), NOT actual current or historical stock prices. Supports multi-symbol queries with parallel fetching for efficient batch analysis and includes robust retry mechanisms for API calls.

 RETURNS: Analyst price targets (predicted future prices), consensus price targets (high/low/median), price target revisions, price target news articles, and analyst-by-analyst target breakdowns.

 USE FOR:
- ONLY when user explicitly asks for price targets or analyst price predictions
- **Valuation Assessment**: Compare current stock price against analyst price targets
- **Sentiment Analysis**: Track analyst sentiment through price target changes
- **Portfolio Screening**: Compare price targets across multiple stocks
- **Market Updates**: Get latest price target news and revisions
- How much upside or downside (analyst-implied return vs current price)

 NOT FOR: Investment based on financials (use get_key_metrics_ratios + ratings_analysis instead) or Good investment evaluation or Actual current or historical stock prices or Revenue/Earnings estimates or Buy/Sell recommendations or What analysts rate X (use stock_grades_analysis for analyst buy/sell/hold ratings).

PARAMETERS:
- **symbol**: CONDITIONAL. Stock ticker symbol(s). Single: 'AAPL', Multiple: 'AAPL,MSFT,GOOGL' or ['AAPL', 'MSFT']. Required for 'summary', 'consensus', 'news' types. Optional for 'latest_news'.
- **type**: REQUIRED. Analysis type - 'summary' (aggregated targets), 'consensus' (current consensus), 'news' (target news), 'latest_news' (recent market updates).
- **start_date**: OPTIONAL. Custom date range start (YYYY-MM-DD). HIGHEST PRIORITY - takes precedence over year and filter_type.
- **end_date**: OPTIONAL. Custom date range end (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with start_date.
- **year**: OPTIONAL. Filter by specific year(s). Single: '2024' or multiple: '2024,2025'. Takes precedence over filter_type.
- **filter_type**: OPTIONAL. Time range filter - 'previous' (past 5 years), 'future' (next 5 years), 'all' (10 years total).

FILTERING PRIORITY: start_date/end_date > year > filter_type > all data

EXAMPLES:
- Multiple stock summary: symbol='AAPL,MSFT,GOOGL', type='summary'
- News with date range: symbol='AAPL', type='news', start_date='2024-01-01', end_date='2024-12-31'
- Consensus: symbol='AAPL', type='consensus'
- Market updates: type='latest_news', filter_type='previous'

 NOTE: Price targets are analyst PREDICTIONS about future stock prices, not actual trading prices.
sample_payloads.name: price_targets_analysis

conditional.symbol: Stock ticker symbol(s). Single: 'AAPL' or Multiple: 'AAPL,MSFT,GOOGL'. REQUIRED for 'summary', 'consensus', 'news' types. OPTIONAL for 'latest_news' (defaults to market-wide data).

optional.end_date: Custom date range end (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with start_date for precise filtering.
optional.filter_type: Time range filter: 'previous' (past 5 years), 'future' (next 5 years), 'all' (10 years total). Lowest priority.
optional.start_date: Custom date range start (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with end_date. HIGHEST PRIORITY - takes precedence over year and filter_type.
optional.year: Filter by specific year(s). Single: '2024' or multiple: '2024,2025'. Takes precedence over filter_type when provided.

required.type: Analysis type: 'summary' (aggregated price targets across time periods), 'consensus' (current consensus with high/low/median), 'news' (price target news articles), 'latest_news' (recent market-wide updates).

filter_type.all: When filter_type='all', returns all available price target data without time restrictions. Provides complete historical view of all analyst price target predictions. This applies to news responses.
filter_type.future: When filter_type='future', filters data to show only price targets from the next 5 years (if available). Rarely used as price targets are forward-looking predictions. This applies to news responses.
filter_type.previous: For previous filter_type, filters data to show only price targets from the past 5 years relative to the current date. Useful for analyzing recent price target trends and historical analyst predictions. This applies to news responses.

type.consensus: Returns current consensus price target data including high, low, median, and consensus (average) targets. Shows the range of current analyst predictions and the consensus view. Ideal for understanding current analyst expectations and the spread between bullish and bearish views.
type.latest_news: Returns recent market-wide price target updates across all stocks (not symbol-specific unless symbol is provided). Shows the most recent price target news and revisions in the market. Useful for staying updated on latest analyst activity across the market.
type.news: Returns individual price target news articles and analyst reports with detailed information including publication dates, analyst names, specific price targets, and news article links. Each record represents one price target publication. Ideal for tracking specific analyst predictions and accessing source materials.
type.summary: Returns aggregated price target statistics across multiple time periods (last month, last quarter, last year, all-time). Provides comprehensive overview of analyst price target activity including counts and average targets for each period. Ideal for understanding overall analyst coverage and consensus trends over different timeframes.

response_example.adjPriceTarget: Adjusted price target after accounting for stock splits, dividends, or other corporate actions, expressed in USD. Ensures price targets remain comparable across different time periods even after corporate actions. Example: 185.00 means the adjusted target is $185. This field is present in news or latest_news responses when adjustments are applicable.
response_example.allTimeAvgPriceTarget: Average (mean) of all analyst price target predictions ever issued for this company throughout its entire coverage history, expressed in USD. Calculated from all historical price targets. Provides long-term consensus view but may be less relevant for current analysis as it includes very old predictions. More useful for historical context than current investment decisions.
response_example.allTimeCount: Total cumulative number of analyst price target predictions ever issued for this company since coverage began. Represents the complete historical count of all price target publications. Higher counts indicate longer analyst coverage history and more established analyst following. Useful for assessing the depth of analyst coverage over the company's public trading history.
response_example.analystCompany: Name of the financial institution, investment bank, or research firm that employs the analyst who issued the price target. Identifies the analyst's employer organization (e.g., 'Goldman Sachs', 'Morgan Stanley', 'JPMorgan'). Useful for assessing institutional credibility and tracking coverage by major financial institutions. This field is present in news or latest_news responses.
response_example.analystName: Name of the individual analyst who issued the price target prediction. Identifies the specific analyst making the forecast. Useful for tracking individual analyst performance and following specific analyst recommendations. This field is present in news or latest_news responses.
response_example.lastMonthAvgPriceTarget: Average (mean) of all analyst price target predictions published in the last 30 days, expressed in USD. Calculated by summing all price targets from the past month and dividing by the count. Represents the consensus price target for the most recent month. Example: 185.50 means analysts expect the stock to reach $185.50 on average over the next 12 months.
response_example.lastMonthCount: Total number of analyst price target predictions issued in the last 30 days (1 month) for this stock. Counts all individual analyst price target publications within the past month. Higher counts indicate more analyst coverage and more frequent price target updates. Useful for assessing analyst interest and coverage frequency.
response_example.lastQuarterAvgPriceTarget: Average (mean) of all analyst price target predictions published in the last 90 days, expressed in USD. Calculated by summing all price targets from the past quarter and dividing by the count. Represents the consensus price target for the most recent quarter. More stable than monthly average as it includes more data points.
response_example.lastQuarterCount: Total number of analyst price target predictions issued in the last 90 days (1 quarter) for this stock. Counts all individual analyst price target publications within the past quarter. Provides broader coverage metric than monthly count, showing analyst activity over a 3-month period.
response_example.lastYearAvgPriceTarget: Average (mean) of all analyst price target predictions published in the last 365 days, expressed in USD. Calculated by summing all price targets from the past year and dividing by the count. Represents the annual consensus price target, providing the most stable average as it includes the most data points over a longer time period.
response_example.lastYearCount: Total number of analyst price target predictions issued in the last 365 days (1 year) for this stock. Counts all individual analyst price target publications within the past year. Provides annual coverage metric showing analyst engagement level over a full year period.
response_example.newsBaseURL: Base domain name of the news publisher or media outlet, used to identify the source of the price target news. Example: 'benzinga.com', 'marketwatch.com'. Useful for verifying source authenticity and identifying reputable publishers. This field is present in news or latest_news responses.
response_example.newsPublisher: Name of the publisher, media outlet, or financial news organization that published the price target news. Identifies the source institution (e.g., 'Benzinga', 'MarketWatch', 'Seeking Alpha'). Useful for assessing source credibility and tracking which outlets are covering the stock. This field is present in news or latest_news responses.
response_example.newsTitle: Headline or title of the news article or analyst report containing the price target. Provides a brief summary of the price target announcement or analysis. Useful for quickly scanning price target updates and understanding the context of the prediction. This field is present in news or latest_news responses.
response_example.newsURL: Full URL link to the news article or analyst report containing the price target information. Provides direct access to the source material for detailed analysis and context. Format: full HTTP/HTTPS URL. Example: 'https://www.benzinga.com/...' This field is present in news or latest_news responses.
response_example.priceTarget: The specific price target prediction made by the analyst, expressed in USD. The exact price the analyst expects the stock to reach within their forecast timeframe (typically 12 months). Example: 185.00 means the analyst predicts the stock will reach $185. This is the analyst's forward-looking price prediction, not the current market price. This field is present in news or latest_news responses.
response_example.priceWhenPosted: Stock price at the exact time when the price target was published or the news article was released, expressed in USD. Shows the market price context when the analyst made their prediction. Useful for calculating the implied upside/downside from the price target. Example: 170.00 means the stock was trading at $170 when the analyst published their target. This field is present in news or latest_news responses.
response_example.publishedDate: Date and time when the price target was published or the news article was released, in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.000Z). Shows exactly when the analyst made the prediction or when the news was published. Useful for tracking price target revisions and understanding the timing of analyst updates. This field is present in news or latest_news responses.
response_example.publishers: Array of publisher names or data sources contributing to the analyst price targets. Lists all the financial institutions, research firms, and media outlets that have published price targets for this stock. Examples may include major investment banks, research firms, and financial news publishers. Useful for identifying which institutions are covering the stock and assessing coverage diversity.
response_example.symbol: Stock ticker symbol for which price target data was retrieved. Single symbol queries return a string (e.g., 'AAPL'). Multi-symbol queries return an array of strings (e.g., ['AAPL', 'MSFT', 'GOOGL']). Each record in the data array contains the symbol field identifying which company the price target applies to.
response_example.targetConsensus: Consensus (average) price target among current analyst predictions, expressed in USD. Calculated as the mean of all current analyst price targets. Represents the market's average expectation for future stock price. Example: 175.00 means analysts on average expect the stock to reach $175. This field is present in consensus responses.
response_example.targetHigh: Highest (most optimistic) price target among current analyst predictions, expressed in USD. Represents the bullish analyst view - the maximum price analysts expect the stock to reach. Example: 200.00 means the most optimistic analyst predicts the stock will reach $200. This field is present in consensus responses.
response_example.targetLow: Lowest (most conservative) price target among current analyst predictions, expressed in USD. Represents the bearish analyst view - the minimum price analysts expect the stock to reach. Example: 150.00 means the most conservative analyst predicts the stock will reach $150. This field is present in consensus responses.
response_example.targetMedian: Median price target among current analyst predictions, expressed in USD. The middle value when all price targets are sorted. Less affected by extreme outliers than the average (consensus). Example: 175.00 means half of analysts predict above $175 and half predict below. This field is present in consensus responses.

sample_payloads.description:
A comprehensive tool to retrieve and analyze analyst grades and recommendations for public companies. Supports multi-symbol queries with parallel fetching for efficient batch analysis and includes robust retry mechanisms and data aggregation capabilities.

WHEN TO USE:
- **Analyst Sentiment**: Track buy/sell/hold recommendations from analysts.
- **Grade Changes**: Monitor upgrades, downgrades, and maintained ratings.
- **Consensus View**: Get aggregated analyst recommendations.
- **Historical Trends**: Analyze grade evolution over time.
- **Portfolio Comparison**: Compare grades across multiple stocks.
- **News & Updates**: Get latest grade-related news.

PARAMETERS:
- **symbol**: CONDITIONAL. Stock ticker symbol(s). Single: 'AAPL', Multiple: 'AAPL,MSFT,GOOGL' or ['AAPL', 'MSFT']. Required for 'current_grades', 'historical_grades', 'consensus', 'news' types. Optional for 'latest_news'.
- **type**: REQUIRED. Analysis type - 'current_grades' (latest), 'historical_grades' (time series), 'consensus' (aggregated), 'news' (grade news), 'latest_news' (recent market updates).
- **start_date**: OPTIONAL. Custom date range start (YYYY-MM-DD). HIGHEST PRIORITY - takes precedence over year and filter_type.
- **end_date**: OPTIONAL. Custom date range end (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with start_date.
- **year**: OPTIONAL. Filter by specific year(s). Single: '2024' or multiple: '2024,2025'. Takes precedence over filter_type.
- **filter_type**: OPTIONAL. Time range filter - 'previous' (past 5 years), 'future' (next 5 years), 'all' (10 years total).
- **aggregation**: OPTIONAL. Control data grouping for historical_grades: 'daily' (no grouping), 'weekly', 'monthly', 'quarterly'. Auto-selects based on data size if not specified.

FILTERING PRIORITY: start_date/end_date > year > filter_type > all data

EXAMPLES:
- Current grades for multiple: symbol='AAPL,MSFT,GOOGL', type='current_grades'
- Historical with date range: symbol='AAPL', type='historical_grades', start_date='2023-01-01', end_date='2024-12-31'
- Historical with aggregation: symbol='AAPL', type='historical_grades', filter_type='all', aggregation='monthly'
- Consensus: symbol='AAPL', type='consensus'
- Market updates: type='latest_news', filter_type='previous'
sample_payloads.name: stock_grades_analysis

conditional.symbol: Stock ticker symbol(s). Single: 'AAPL' or Multiple: 'AAPL,MSFT,GOOGL'. REQUIRED for 'current_grades', 'historical_grades', 'consensus', 'news' types. OPTIONAL for 'latest_news' (defaults to market-wide data).

optional.aggregation: Control data grouping for historical_grades: 'daily' (no grouping), 'weekly', 'monthly', 'quarterly'. Auto-selects based on data size if not specified.
optional.end_date: Custom date range end (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with start_date for precise filtering.
optional.filter_type: Time range filter: 'previous' (past 5 years), 'future' (next 5 years), 'all' (10 years total). Lowest priority.
optional.start_date: Custom date range start (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with end_date. HIGHEST PRIORITY - takes precedence over year and filter_type.
optional.year: Filter by specific year(s). Single: '2024' or multiple: '2024,2025'. Takes precedence over filter_type when provided.

required.type: Analysis type: 'current_grades' (latest analyst grades), 'historical_grades' (grade evolution over time), 'consensus' (aggregated recommendations), 'news' (grade-related news), 'latest_news' (recent market updates).

response_example.action: The action taken by the analyst or institution, Example: "maintain". (String)

response_example.analystRatingsHold: The number of analysts who rated the stock as "Hold". Example: 14. (Number)
response_example.analystRatingsSell: The number of analysts who rated the stock as "Sell". Example: 2. (Number)

response_example.buy: The number of analysts who rated the stock as "Buy". Example: 29. (Number)

response_example.date: The date associated with the grade or recommendation. Example: "2025-01-21". (String, Date)
response_example.gradeChange: The change in grade (upgrade, downgrade, or maintained). Example: "Upgrade". (String)
response_example.gradingCompany: The company or institution that issued the grade. Example: "Goldman Sachs". (String)
response_example.hold: The number of analysts who rated the stock as "Hold". Example: 11. (Number)

response_example.publishedDate: The date and time when the news article or analyst report was published, in ISO 8601 format. Example: "2025-01-31T10:11:47.000Z". (String, DateTime)

sample_payloads.api_name: CoinGecko Trending Pools by Network API
sample_payloads.description:
Find trending liquidity pools on a specific blockchain network. Returns hottest DEX pools on Ethereum, Solana, Base, or other networks based on recent trading activity and user engagement. Use this when you want network-specific trending analysis. Each pool includes token pair, 24h volume, transaction count, TVL, price changes (5m, 1h, 6h, 24h), buy/sell activity, and DEX info. Automatically fetches multiple pages (20 pools per page, default 100 pools). Perfect for focused network analysis and discovering hot pairs on your preferred blockchain.

When to use:
- Find trending pools on Ethereum
- Discover hot Solana pairs
- Track trending Base network pools
- Monitor Polygon DeFi momentum
- Focus on specific blockchain ecosystem

Parameters:
- network: Blockchain network (required). Examples: 'eth', 'solana', 'base', 'polygon', 'arbitrum', 'optimism'.
- duration: Trending timeframe (optional, default: '24h'). Options: '5m', '1h', '6h', '24h'.
- max_pages: Pages to fetch (optional, default: 5 = 100 pools).
- include: Additional data (optional, default: 'base_token').
sample_payloads.name: trending_pools_network

optional.max_pages: Pages to fetch (optional, default: 5 = 100 pools). Set to -1 for all pools.

required.network: Blockchain network ID (required). Examples: 'eth' for Ethereum, 'solana' for Solana, 'base' for Base, 'polygon' for Polygon, 'arbitrum' for Arbitrum, 'optimism' for Optimism.

response_example.data: Pool records with pair, volume, TVL, price changes. (Array)

sample_payloads.description: Generates formal account statements for specified time periods. Displays opening balance, closing balance, total deposits, total withdrawals, interest earned, fees charged, number of transactions, and net change. Produces monthly, quarterly, yearly, or custom period statements suitable for financial records and documentation.
sample_payloads.description_ar:
ينشئ كشوفات حساب رسمية لفترات زمنية محددة. يعرض الرصيد الافتتاحي، الرصيد الختامي، إجمالي الإيداعات، إجمالي السحوبات، الفوائد المكتسبة، الرسوم المحصلة، عدد المعاملات، والتغيير الصافي. ينتج كشوفات شهرية، ربع سنوية، سنوية، أو فترات مخصصة مناسبة للسجلات والوثائق المالية.

استخدم عندما:
- كشوفات حساب رسمية (كشف الحساب، بيان الحساب)
- ملخصات فترية (كشف شهري، كشف ربع سنوي، كشف سنوي)
- تحليل الرصيد والمعاملات (الرصيد الافتتاحي، الرصيد الختامي، عدد المعاملات)
- السجلات المالية (الإيداعات، السحوبات، الفوائد، الرسوم)

كلمات مفتاحية: كشف الحساب، بيان الحساب، كشف شهري، كشف سنوي، رصيد افتتاحي، رصيد ختامي، معاملات الحساب
sample_payloads.name: statements

optional.account_id: Specific account ID to generate statement for (default: all user accounts). Filters statement to single account.
optional.end_date: End date for custom period statement in YYYY-MM-DD format. Only used when period='custom'. Defines the end of the statement period.
optional.period: Statement period (default: 'last_month'). Options: 'current_month' (month to date), 'last_month' (previous month), 'last_3_months' (last 3 months), 'last_6_months' (last 6 months), 'ytd' (year to date), 'custom' (requires start_date and end_date).
optional.start_date: Start date for custom period statement in YYYY-MM-DD format. Only used when period='custom'. Defines the beginning of the statement period.

response_example.account_statements: Account statements array. Each statement includes: account_id (integer), account_name (localized string), account_type (string - 'checking'/'savings'/'credit_card'/'brokerage_cash'), currency (string - 'SAR'), opened_at (date - account opening date), closed_at (date or null - account closing date), opening_balance (decimal - balance at period start), closing_balance (decimal - balance at period end), total_credits (decimal - sum of all deposits/credits in period), total_debits (decimal - sum of all withdrawals/debits in period), interest_earned (decimal - interest accrued in period), fees_charged (decimal - fees charged in period), days_in_period (integer - number of days covered), transaction_count (integer - total number of transactions in period), net_change (decimal - calculated as total_credits minus total_debits). Example: [{account_id: 12345, account_name: 'My Checking Account', account_type: 'checking', currency: 'SAR', opened_at: '2023-01-15', closed_at: null, opening_balance: 15000.00, closing_balance: 18500.00, total_credits: 12000.00, total_debits: 8500.00, interest_earned: 0.00, fees_charged: 0.00, days_in_period: 31, transaction_count: 45, net_change: 3500.00}]. (Array of Objects)
response_example.statement_period: Statement period in ISO format. Example: '2024-10-01 to 2024-10-31'. Shows the exact date range covered by the statement. (String)
response_example.statement_period_display: Human-readable statement period. Example: 'October 2024' for single month. Formatted based on language preference. (String)

sample_payloads.description: Compares current spending with previous months and forecasts spending for remainder of current period. Generates conservative, normal, and optimistic spending scenarios with probability percentages. Shows month-over-month changes, spending pace relative to month-end projection, and historical baseline averages. Provides category-wise spending estimates with trend indicators showing increases or decreases.
sample_payloads.description_ar:
يقارن الإنفاق الحالي مع الأشهر السابقة ويتوقع الإنفاق لباقي الفترة الحالية. يولد سيناريوهات إنفاق محافظة، عادية، ومتفائلة مع نسب احتمالية. يوضح التغييرات شهر بعد شهر، وتيرة الإنفاق بالنسبة لتوقعات نهاية الشهر، ومتوسطات الأساس التاريخية. يوفر تقديرات الإنفاق حسب الفئة مع مؤشرات الاتجاهات التي تظهر الزيادات أو النقصان.

استخدم عندما:
- مقارنة الإنفاق الحالي مع الأشهر السابقة
- توقعات الإنفاق لباقي الفترة
- سيناريوهات الإنفاق المختلفة (محافظ/عادي/متفائل)
- تحليل التغييرات شهرية والوتيرة
- تقديرات حسب الفئة مع اتجاهات الزيادة/النقصان

كلمات مفتاحية: مقارنة الإنفاق، توقعات الإنفاق، سيناريوهات الإنفاق، تحليل الاتجاهات، تقديرات الفئات
sample_payloads.name: estimate_my_spending

optional.comparison_months: Number of previous months to compare (1-12, default: 3). Determines historical baseline period for comparison.
optional.include_scenarios: Include Conservative/Normal/Optimistic spending scenarios (default: true). Generates three probability-based spending projections.
optional.include_weekly_projections: Include week-by-week spending projections (default: true). Shows weekly spending patterns and projections.
optional.seasonal_adjustment: Apply seasonal factors for holidays and events (default: true). Adjusts for Ramadan, Eid, summer, and other seasonal patterns.

response_example.category_estimates: Category-wise spending estimates array.
